sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk appetite remains constructive as Asian markets digest overnight US tech strength and stabilizing European momentum. The session is characterized by modest dollar weakness against commodity currencies, divergent equity performance with Nikkei underperforming, and steady crypto gains. Central bank policy normalization expectations continue to drive cross-asset volatility as markets position ahead of key data releases.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
1. CHF (+0.08%) - Safe haven flows amid European political uncertainty
2. AUD (+0.07%) - Commodity strength supporting risk-sensitive currency
3. NZD (+0.03%) - Following broader commodity currency momentum
4. CAD (-0.01%) - Oil gains offset by broader dollar resilience
5. GBP (-0.01%) - Consolidating near session highs
6. JPY (-0.01%) - Intervention concerns limiting further weakness
7. EUR (-0.03%) - Political fragmentation weighing on single currency

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1714: Bearish bias below 1.1750 resistance. ECB dovish pivot expectations and political uncertainty in core eurozone maintaining downward pressure. Support at 1.1680.
USD/JPY 157.8390: Approaching critical intervention zone. BOJ verbal warnings intensifying above 158.00 handle. Risk of coordinated intervention limiting upside despite yield differentials.
AUD/USD 0.7263: Constructive above 0.7250 support. Iron ore strength and RBA hawkish stance supporting. Targeting 0.7300 resistance on commodity momentum.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Real money EUR selling continues on political fragmentation concerns
• Commodity currency positioning turning constructive on China reopening optimism
• JPY intervention hedges building as 158.00 approaches
• Systematic rebalancing flows supporting USD across majors

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: Tech leadership driving US outperformance: Nasdaq +1.20%, S&P 500 +0.56%, while Dow -0.14% reflects old economy weakness. European bourses solid with DAX +0.76%, FTSE +0.58%, CAC +0.35%. Nikkei -0.70% underperforms on intervention fears and tech profit-taking. Sector rotation favoring growth over value continues.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold $4701.60: Consolidating below $4720 resistance. Central bank buying supporting despite higher real yields. Key support $4680.
WTI Crude $101.46: Testing $102 resistance on supply concerns. OPEC+ compliance and geopolitical premiums supportive. Bullish above $100.
Silver $87.70: Sharp -1.87% decline on industrial demand concerns. Critical support at $87.00.
Natural Gas $2.884: Weather-driven gains continuing. European storage levels remain supportive.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC $79834 (+0.69%): Consolidating above $79000 support with $82000 resistance in focus. Institutional flows remain constructive despite regulatory overhang.
ETH $2270.29 (+0.56%): ETH/BTC ratio stabilizing. Shanghai upgrade benefits continuing to support relative performance versus Bitcoin.
Top movers: DOGE $0.1145 (+1.60%) leading meme coin recovery. XRP $1.4342 (+0.54%) and SOL $91.25 (+0.18%) showing steady institutional accumulation.

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
• AUD/USD targeting 0.7300 on commodity strength
• Natural Gas on weather premium continuation
• Nasdaq on tech momentum persistence
• BTC above $79000 support for $82000 test
📉 SHORT
• EUR/USD below 1.1750 on political uncertainty
• Silver on industrial demand concerns
• Nikkei on intervention risk premium
• USD/JPY above 158.00 on intervention threat

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: US Retail Sales and Industrial Production data key for Fed policy expectations. ECB speakers monitoring political developments. 10-year yields at critical technical levels across majors.

quote:
🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session

Risk appetite remains constructive as Asian markets digest overnight US tech strength and stabilizing European momentum
conclusion: US Retail Sales and Industrial Production data key for Fed policy expectations. ECB speakers monitoring political developments. 10-year yields at critical technical levels across majors.
